claim_short: 1210.3401 Recent progress on Monotone Operator Theory open problems in monotone operator theory asks: Let A,B:X=>X* be maximally monotone with dom A cap int dom B nonempty. Assume A is type (FPV). Is A+B necessarily maximally monotone? Literature-implied partial subcase: 1305.6691 Borwein Yao Sum theorems for maximally monotone operators of type (FPV) proves Theorem 4.2 under star(dom A) cap int dom B nonempty and...
notes: registry_status=literature_implied_answer (partial subcase: FPV sum problem with convex domain)
packet_title: Literature-Implied Answer: FPV Sum Problem With Convex Domain
source_question: Source paper: J. M. Borwein and L. Yao, *Recent progress on Monotone Operator Theory*, arXiv:1210.3401. Location: Section "Open problems in Monotone Operator Theory", p. 33 of the arXiv PDF. Source question: Let A, B: X -> X* be maximally monotone with dom A cap int dom B != empty. Assume that A is of type (FPV). Is A+B necessarily maximally monotone?
source_title: Recent progress on Monotone Operator Theory
